info_gaps: last-known-well time for any neuro change unclear

resource.note: only 1 monitored bed remaining — likely candidate for this patient

warnings: Vitals reassuring but gestalt is alarming — ashen, diaphoretic, drowsy, altered behavior per wife
Anginal-equivalent presentation possible (diaphoresis + weakness in 64yo HTN male)
Altered mental status meets ESI-2 high-risk criteria independent of vitals
Only 1 monitored bed left — allocate before next 'now' patient arrives

hypotheses.name: Sepsis / occult infection
supporting_signals: altered mental status (drowsy, slow to answer)

hypotheses.name: Acute stroke / posterior circulation
supporting_signals: drowsy, slow to answer, won't make eye contact
sudden change per wife ('this is not him')

hypotheses.name: Occult hemorrhage / GI bleed / AAA
rules_against: HR 76 — not tachycardic (though possibly blunted on amlodipine? amlodipine is CCB not beta-blocker)

immediate.action: Place in monitored bed; 12-lead ECG within door-to-ECG 10-min target, cardiac monitor, IV access, fingerstick glucose
immediate.reason: Anginal-equivalent presentation in 64yo HTN male with diaphoresis and altered behavior — rule out ACS now.

immediate.action: MD bedside evaluation now: full neuro exam (NIHSS-style), mental status, focused cardiac/abdominal exam
immediate.reason: Altered mental status (drowsy, slow responses) meets ESI-2 high-risk criterion; need to differentiate ACS vs stroke vs sepsis vs occult bleed.

immediate.action: Draw labs: troponin, CBC, BMP, lactate, coags, type & screen; consider CT head if any focal finding on exam
immediate.reason: Broad workup warranted given undifferentiated 'sick' presentation with mental status change; lactate screens for occult sepsis/shock.

monitor.action: Continuous cardiac monitoring + repeat vitals q5min until stable workup back
monitor.reason: Vitals currently normal but clinical picture concerning — trend will reveal evolving shock, arrhythmia, or hypoxia.

monitor.action: Repeat ECG and troponin at 3h if first set negative and symptoms persist
monitor.reason: Single negative troponin/ECG does not exclude evolving ACS in anginal-equivalent presentation.

escalate_or_redirect.action: Notify cardiology on-call (ETA 15 min) for early consult pending ECG/troponin
escalate_or_redirect.reason: Conservative bias — undifferentiated 'gray and unwell' 64yo with HTN warrants early specialty input; closest alternative tier was 'soon' but gestalt + age + AMS pushed to 'now'.

explanation.summary: 64yo male with HTN presenting with vague weakness, ashen/diaphoretic appearance, and acute mental status change (drowsy, slow to respond, behavior change per wife). Vitals are reassuring but the constellation — pallor, diaphoresis, AMS in an older HTN male — is a classic anginal-equivalent / occult critical illness presentation. Triaging NOW (ESI-2) on basis of high-risk situation and altered mental status, even though no single vital crosses a red-flag threshold. Conservative bias applied: gestalt overrides reassuring numbers.

citations.signal: altered mental status (drowsy, slow to answer) + high-risk gestalt
age ≥40, HTN, diaphoresis as anginal equivalent — door-to-ECG ≤10 min

checklist.task: Obtain 12-lead ECG and place on cardiac monitor
IV access x2, draw troponin/CBC/BMP/lactate/coags/type & screen
MD bedside eval with focused neuro + cardiac exam
Allocate the last monitored bed to this patient
Page cardiology on-call for early consult
Repeat vitals q5min until workup resulted
Repeat ECG + troponin at 3h if initial negative

why_this_tier: Altered mental status (drowsy, slow to answer, behavioral change per family) independently meets ESI-2 high-risk criterion
Diaphoresis + ashen appearance + age 64 + HTN is a classic anginal-equivalent ACS pattern
Family/nurse gestalt ('this is not him', 'something is very wrong') is a known high-yield signal in occult critical illness
Conservative bias applied when vitals reassure but presentation alarms
would_downgrade_if: ECG normal AND troponin negative AND glucose normal AND mental status returns to baseline within 30 min
Wife confirms this is patient's chronic baseline (e.g., post-ictal pattern, known dementia)
Clear benign explanation emerges (vasovagal, dehydration with rapid recovery)
would_upgrade_if: Any ischemic ECG change or elevated troponin
Any focal neuro deficit on exam
Lactate ≥4 or any sign of evolving shock
